reports.title: The Siege That Talks Are Supposed to End
reports.subtitle: The US blockade of Iranian ports is working militarily and collapsing diplomatically at the same time.

reports.title: The Justice Who Said What the Court Won't
reports.subtitle: Ketanji Brown Jackson's Yale speech against the shadow docket is not just dissent: it is a warning about what the Court has already become.

reports.title: Tariffs Struck Down, Tariffs Coming Back
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court ruled Trump's emergency tariff powers unconstitutional. The administration is already routing around the ruling.

reports.title: Banned by the Government, Worth $800 Billion
reports.subtitle: The Pentagon tried to kill Anthropic's federal business. Instead it handed the company a brand identity and a revenue surge.

reports.title: Five Countries, One Law They Cannot Write
reports.subtitle: Canada, Australia, the EU, and multiple US states are all trying to ban social media for minors. None of them know how to enforce it.

reports.title: The Deal That Depends on Beijing Keeping Its Word
reports.subtitle: Trump says China agreed not to arm Iran. That claim is doing enormous diplomatic work for a ceasefire that does not yet exist.

reports.title: The IMF Is Describing a Recession. Bessent Is Calling It a Strategy.
reports.subtitle: The IMF cut global growth forecasts and warned the Iran war could tip the world into recession. The US Treasury Secretary told the BBC the pain is worth it.

reports.title: Jensen Huang Said There Was No Evidence. There Were Six Indictments.
reports.subtitle: Federal prosecutors have charged six men in three weeks with smuggling billions in Nvidia chips to China. The CEO who denied it is now selling chips to the US government instead.

reports.title: The DOJ Just Said Trump Can Burn His Papers
reports.subtitle: The Justice Department declared the Presidential Records Act unconstitutional. The law was passed because a president tried to do exactly this before.

reports.title: Courts Are Making Climate Policy Because Governments Won't
reports.subtitle: The ICJ's 2025 advisory opinion is now triggering binding rulings from The Hague to the Caribbean. The Dutch government is appealing. Governments worldwide are watching who wins.

reports.title: The First State to Say No to AI
reports.subtitle: Maine just passed the nation's first statewide moratorium on AI data centers. The governor hasn't signed it yet, and that's where the real story begins.

reports.title: The Fourth Time Is Not the Charm
reports.subtitle: Senate Republicans blocked the Iran war powers resolution again. But the 60-day War Powers Act clock is ticking, and their private words no longer match their public votes.

reports.title: He Said It Was Defeated
reports.subtitle: In January, Trump told G7 leaders inflation was beaten. The IMF now projects the US will have the worst inflation of any G7 country in 2026.

reports.title: The Waiver Is Gone. India Is Next.
reports.subtitle: Bessent just ended the sanctions relief that let India buy Russian and Iranian oil. He also threatened secondary sanctions on the banks that helped them do it.

reports.title: Anthropic Built a Weapon. Then It Built a Cage.
reports.subtitle: Claude Opus 4.7 launched today with a built-in cybersecurity block. Anthropic admits it deliberately reduced the model's offensive capabilities. The question is whether deliberate reduction is enough.
